content.text:
<objective>
Create executable phase prompts (PLAN.md files) for a roadmap phase with integrated research and verification.

**Default flow:** Research (if needed) → Plan → Verify → Done

**Orchestrator role:** Parse arguments, validate phase, research domain (unless skipped), spawn gsd-planner, verify with gsd-plan-checker, iterate until pass or max iterations, present results.
</objective>

<execution_context>
@/Users/baotoq/.claude/get-shit-done/workflows/plan-phase.md
@/Users/baotoq/.claude/get-shit-done/references/ui-brand.md
</execution_context>

<context>
Phase number: 35 --auto (optional — auto-detects next unplanned phase if omitted)

**Flags:**
- `--research` — Force re-research even if RESEARCH.md exists
- `--skip-research` — Skip research, go straight to planning
- `--gaps` — Gap closure mode (reads VERIFICATION.md, skips research)
- `--skip-verify` — Skip verification loop
- `--prd <file>` — Use a PRD/acceptance criteria file instead of discuss-phase. Parses requirements into CONTEXT.md automatically. Skips discuss-phase entirely.

Normalize phase input in step 2 before any directory lookups.
</context>

<process>
Execute the plan-phase workflow from @/Users/baotoq/.claude/get-shit-done/workflows/plan-phase.md end-to-end.
Preserve all workflow gates (validation, research, planning, verification loop, routing).
</process>
I'll initialize the plan-phase workflow for phase 35.

input.command:
INIT=$(node "$HOME/.claude/get-shit-done/bin/gsd-tools.cjs" init plan-phase "35")
if [[ "$INIT" == @file:* ]]; then INIT=$(cat "${INIT#@file:}"); fi
echo "$INIT"

<planning_context>

**Phase description:**
### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces

**Project instructions:** Read ./CLAUDE.md if exists — follow project-specific guidelines
**Project skills:** Check .claude/skills/ or .agents/skills/ directory (if either exists) — read SKILL.md files, plans should account for project skill rules
</planning_context>

- Verification criteria
- must_haves for goal-backward verification
</downstream_consumer>

    56→## Accumulated Context
    57→
    58→### Decisions
    59→
    60→- [30-01] Aspire pinned via SDK package ref in csproj; CI does not install workload
    61→- [30-01] Release workflow uses workflow_call test gate with needs: [tests]
    62→- [30-01] Dockerfile uses ARG (not ENV) for build-time auth placeholders
    63→- [31-01] Top-level permissions: {} on release.yml with job-level overrides for deny-by-default
    64→- [31-01] Inclusive paths filter (not paths-ignore) for explicit allowlist of trigger paths
    65→- [31-01] NuGet cache key uses both csproj and Directory.Packages.props hashes
    66→- [32-01] Namespace set only via Kustomize transformer in kustomization.yaml, never in individual manifests
    67→- [32-01] otel-collector and aspire-dashboard stay in base/ directory but referenced only from dev overlay
    68→- [Phase 32]: Retained existing app: X labels alongside app.kubernetes.io/* for backward compatibility
    69→- [Phase 32]: All containers use imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent (no latest tags in use)
    70→- [Phase 32]: Service selectors keep only app: X to avoid restrictive pod matching
    71→- [Phase 33]: postgres uses runAsNonRoot: false because official image manages privilege dropping via gosu
    72→- [Phase 33]: postgres/keycloak/rabbitmq use readOnlyRootFilesystem: false due to runtime write requirements
    73→- [Phase 33]: securityContext at container level (not pod level) for granular control
    74→- [33-02] Web secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) use secretKeyRef to web-secrets SealedSecret
    75→- [33-02] Keycloak base uses 'start' (production); dev overlay patches to 'start-dev' via strategic merge
    76→- [Phase 34]: docker added to prerequisite checks alongside kind/kubectl/kubeseal since needed for image builds
    77→- [Phase 34]: Context guard placed after cluster creation so kind sets context before verification
    78→- [Phase 34]: Identical outbox config across all DbContexts (1s QueryDelay, 5min DuplicateDetectionWindow)
    79→- [Phase 34]: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL returns empty string in Aspire (same-origin), http://localhost:38800 in K8s
    80→- [Phase 34]: RabbitMQ uses StatefulSet with headless service and 1Gi PVC for message persistence
    81→- [Phase 34]: Startup probe timeouts tuned per service: RabbitMQ 130s, gateway/web/aspire 65s, otel-collector 33s
    82→

    71→### v3.1 K8s & GitOps Hardening
    72→
    73→**Milestone Goal:** Fix critical security, reliability, and CI/CD issues identified in the v3.0 implementation audit.
    74→
    75→- [x] **Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes** - Restore broken workflows with correct SDK, paths, and test gates (completed 2026-03-08)
    76→- [x] **Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening** - Secure and optimize CI with permissions, caching, and path filters (completed 2026-03-08)
    77→- [x] **Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene** - Clean up K8s manifests with standard labels, image policies, and overlay structure (completed 2026-03-08)
    78→- [x] **Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening** - Lock down workloads with security contexts, service accounts, and sealed secrets (completed 2026-03-08)
    79→- [x] **Phase 34: Reliability Improvements** - Harden runtime with persistent messaging, startup probes, and bootstrap safety (completed 2026-03-08)
    80→- [ ] **Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices** - Enforce deployment ordering, seal secrets in Git, and scope ArgoCD access
    81→
    82→## Phase Details
    83→
    84→### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
    85→**Goal**: CI workflows pass on push to master — tests run, images build, releases work
    86→**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase — CI is broken, fix it first)
    87→**Requirements**: CICD-01, CICD-02, CICD-03, CICD-07, CICD-08
    88→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    89→  1. `dotnet-test.yml` workflow passes with .NET 10 SDK and current project paths
    90→  2. `release.yml` builds and publishes images using correct project references
    91→  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing
    92→  4. Dockerfile uses ARG for build-time placeholder secrets (no ENV leaking to runtime)
    93→  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
    94→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
    95→Plans:
    96→- [ ] 30-01-PLAN.md — Fix all CI/CD workflows (SDK, paths, test gate, Aspire, Dockerfile secrets)
    97→
    98→### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
    99→**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 30
   101→**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
   104→  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
   105→  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
   106→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
   107→Plans:
   108→- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows
   109→
   110→### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
   111→**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
   112→**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
   113→**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
   114→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   115→  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
   116→  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
   117→  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
   118→  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
   119→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   120→Plans:
   121→- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
   122→- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads
   123→
   124→### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
   125→**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
   126→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
   127→**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04
   128→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   129→  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
   130→  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
   131→  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
   132→  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
   133→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   134→Plans:
   135→- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
   136→- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay
   137→
   138→### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
   139→**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
   140→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
   141→**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
   142→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   143→  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
   144→  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
   145→  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
   146→  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
   147→  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
   148→  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
   149→  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
   150→**Plans:** 3/3 plans complete
   151→Plans:
   152→- [ ] 34-01-PLAN.md — Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler)
   153→- [ ] 34-02-PLAN.md — Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, and startup probes
   154→- [ ] 34-03-PLAN.md — MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts
   155→
   156→### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
   157→**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
   158→**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
   159→**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
   160→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   161→  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
   162→  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
   163→  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
   164→  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
   165→  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
   166→  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces
   167→**Plans**: TBD
   168→

1→# Requirements: MicroCommerce
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-08
     4→**Core Value:** A user can complete a purchase end-to-end — deployed to Kubernetes via GitOps
     5→
     6→## v3.1 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for K8s & GitOps Hardening. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### CI/CD
    11→
    12→- [x] **CICD-01**: .NET SDK version updated to 10.0.x in dotnet-test.yml and release.yml
    13→- [x] **CICD-02**: Stale project paths in release.yml updated to current structure (MicroCommerce.ApiService, MicroCommerce.Gateway)
    14→- [x] **CICD-03**: Aspire workload install pinned to specific version
    15→- [x] **CICD-04**: Explicit least-privilege `permissions:` block on all workflows
    16→- [x] **CICD-05**: NuGet package caching added to test and image build workflows
    17→- [x] **CICD-06**: Path filtering on container-images.yml to skip non-source changes
    18→- [x] **CICD-07**: Tests re-enabled as gate in release.yml
    19→- [x] **CICD-08**: Dockerfile uses ARG instead of ENV for build-time placeholder secrets
    20→
    21→### Security
    22→
    23→- [x] **SEC-01**: Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) moved to SealedSecret
    24→- [x] **SEC-02**: securityContext added to all 8 workloads (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities)
    25→- [x] **SEC-03**: Keycloak base manifest uses `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
    26→- [x] **SEC-04**: Dedicated ServiceAccounts per workload with automountServiceAccountToken: false
    27→- [ ] **SEC-05**: Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject with restricted sourceRepos and destinations
    28→
    29→### ArgoCD
    30→
    31→- [ ] **ARGO-01**: Sync wave annotations on all Application resources enforcing dependency ordering
    32→- [ ] **ARGO-02**: Sealed secret YAML files committed to Git and added as Kustomize resources
    33→- [ ] **ARGO-03**: Root app retry strategy matching child app configuration
    34→- [ ] **ARGO-04**: ignoreDifferences configured for PostgreSQL StatefulSet with RespectIgnoreDifferences
    35→- [ ] **ARGO-05**: Consistent overlay paths for all applications (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
    36→
    37→### Reliability
    38→
    39→- [x] **REL-01**: Client-side API URL resolution returns browser-reachable URL in K8s
    40→- [x] **REL-02**: RabbitMQ converted to StatefulSet with persistent volume
    41→- [x] **REL-03**: Startup probes added to gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, aspire-dashboard
    42→- [x] **REL-04**: Bootstrap script checks for required CLI tools before execution
    43→- [x] **REL-05**: Bootstrap script adds trap handler for failure cleanup guidance
    44→- [x] **REL-06**: Bootstrap script context guard prevents applying to wrong cluster
    45→- [x] **REL-07**: MassTransit outbox extended to cover all DbContexts that publish domain events
    46→
    47→### Kustomize Hygiene
    48→
    49→- [x] **KUST-01**: Hardcoded namespaces removed from base manifests (Kustomize transformer handles it)
    50→- [x] **KUST-02**: otel-collector and aspire-dashboard added to dev overlay
    51→- [x] **KUST-03**: Explicit imagePullPolicy on all application deployments
    52→- [x] **KUST-04**: Standard Kubernetes labels (app.kubernetes.io/*) on all workloads
    53→
    54→## Future Requirements
    55→
    56→None deferred — all audit findings scoped to v3.1.
    57→
    58→## Out of Scope
    59→
    60→| Feature | Reason |
    61→|---------|--------|
    62→| Network policies | Low priority for local dev kind cluster |
    63→| PodDisruptionBudgets | Single-replica dev; add when scaling |
    64→| Pod anti-affinity/topology spread | Irrelevant for single-node kind cluster |
    65→| ResourceQuota/LimitRange | Resource limits already on all containers |
    66→| Prometheus scrape annotations | OTEL Collector handles observability |
    67→| Next.js frontend OTEL instrumentation | Separate concern, not v3.0 hardening |
    68→| Random generated credentials in bootstrap | Dev-only kind cluster; document the pattern |
    69→| Pin image digests (node, postgres, etc.) | Dependabot handles version updates |

    19→  - Bash(argocd:*, kubectl:*)
    20→  - Glob
    21→  - Grep
    22→requirements:
    23→  argocd: ">=2.9"
    24→  kubernetes: ">=1.28"
    25→---
    26→
    27→# ArgoCD Expert
    28→
    29→You are an expert in ArgoCD with deep knowledge of GitOps workflows, application deployment, sync strategies, RBAC, and production operations. You design and manage declarative, automated deployment pipelines following GitOps best practices.
    30→
    31→## Core Expertise
    32→
    33→### ArgoCD Architecture
    34→
    35→**Components:**
    36→```
    37→ArgoCD:
    38→├── API Server (UI/CLI/API)
    39→├── Repository Server (Git interaction)
    40→├── Application Controller (K8s reconciliation)
    41→├── Redis (caching)
    42→├── Dex (SSO/RBAC)
    43→└── ApplicationSet Controller (multi-cluster)
    44→```
    45→
    46→### Installation
    47→
    48→**Install ArgoCD:**
    49→```bash
    50→# Create namespace
    51→kubectl create namespace argocd
    52→
    53→# Install ArgoCD
    54→kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/install.yaml
    55→
    56→# Install with HA
    57→kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/ha/install.yaml
    58→
    59→# Get admin password
    60→kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d
    61→
    62→# Port forward to access UI
    63→kubectl port-forward svc/argocd-server -n argocd 8080:443
    64→
    65→# Login via CLI
    66→argocd login localhost:8080 --username admin --password <password>
    67→
    68→# Change admin password
    69→argocd account update-password
    70→```
    71→
    72→**Production Installation with Custom Values:**
    73→```yaml
    74→# argocd-values.yaml
    75→apiVersion: v1
    76→kind: ConfigMap
    77→metadata:
    78→  name: argocd-cm
    79→  namespace: argocd
    80→data:
    81→  # Repository credentials
    82→  repositories: |
    83→    - url: https://github.com/myorg/myrepo
    84→      passwordSecret:
    85→        name: github-secret
    86→        key: password
    87→      usernameSecret:
    88→        name: github-secret
    89→        key: username
    90→
    91→  # Resource customizations
    92→  resource.customizations: |
    93→    networking.k8s.io/Ingress:
    94→      health.lua: |
    95→        hs = {}
    96→        hs.status = "Healthy"
    97→        return hs
    98→
    99→  # Timeout settings
   100→  timeout.reconciliation: 180s
   101→
   102→  # Diff customizations
   103→  resource.compareoptions: |
   104→    ignoreAggregatedRoles: true
   105→
   106→  # UI customization
   107→  ui.cssurl: "https://cdn.example.com/custom.css"
   108→```
   109→
   110→### Application CRD
   111→
   112→**Basic Application:**
   113→```yaml
   114→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   115→kind: Application
   116→metadata:
   117→  name: myapp
   118→  namespace: argocd
   119→  finalizers:
   120→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   121→spec:
   122→  project: production
   123→
   124→  source:
   125→    repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   126→    targetRevision: main
   127→    path: k8s/overlays/production
   128→
   129→  destination:
   130→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   131→    namespace: production
   132→
   133→  syncPolicy:
   134→    automated:
   135→      prune: true
   136→      selfHeal: true
   137→      allowEmpty: false
   138→    syncOptions:
   139→    - CreateNamespace=true
   140→    retry:
   141→      limit: 5
   142→      backoff:
   143→        duration: 5s
   144→        factor: 2
   145→        maxDuration: 3m
   146→```
   147→
   148→**Helm Application:**
   149→```yaml
   150→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   151→kind: Application
   152→metadata:
   153→  name: myapp-helm
   154→  namespace: argocd
   155→spec:
   156→  project: production
   157→
   158→  source:
   159→    repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/helm-charts
   160→    targetRevision: main
   161→    path: charts/myapp
   162→    helm:
   163→      releaseName: myapp
   164→      valueFiles:
   165→      - values.yaml
   166→      - values-production.yaml
   167→      parameters:
   168→      - name: image.tag
   169→        value: "v2.0.0"
   170→      - name: replicaCount
   171→        value: "5"
   172→      values: |
   173→        ingress:
   174→          enabled: true
   175→          hosts:
   176→          - myapp.example.com
   177→
   178→  destination:
   179→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   180→    namespace: production
   181→
   182→  syncPolicy:
   183→    automated:
   184→      prune: true
   185→      selfHeal: true
   186→    syncOptions:
   187→    - CreateNamespace=true
   188→```
   189→
   190→**Kustomize Application:**
   191→```yaml
   192→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   193→kind: Application
   194→metadata:
   195→  name: myapp-kustomize
   196→  namespace: argocd
   197→spec:
   198→  project: production
   199→
   200→  source:
   201→    repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   202→    targetRevision: main
   203→    path: k8s/overlays/production
   204→    kustomize:
   205→      namePrefix: prod-
   206→      nameSuffix: -v2
   207→      images:
   208→      - myregistry.io/myapp:v2.0.0
   209→      commonLabels:
   210→        environment: production
   211→      commonAnnotations:
   212→        managed-by: argocd
   213→
   214→  destination:
   215→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   216→    namespace: production
   217→
   218→  syncPolicy:
   219→    automated:
   220→      prune: true
   221→      selfHeal: true
   222→```
   223→
   224→### AppProject
   225→
   226→**Project with RBAC:**
   227→```yaml
   228→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   229→kind: AppProject
   230→metadata:
   231→  name: production
   232→  namespace: argocd
   233→spec:
   234→  description: Production applications
   235→
   236→  # Source repositories
   237→  sourceRepos:
   238→  - https://github.com/myorg/*
   239→  - https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
   240→
   241→  # Destination clusters and namespaces
   242→  destinations:
   243→  - namespace: production
   244→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   245→  - namespace: monitoring
   246→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   247→
   248→  # Cluster resource whitelist
   249→  clusterResourceWhitelist:
   250→  - group: '*'
   251→    kind: '*'
   252→
   253→  # Namespace resource blacklist
   254→  namespaceResourceBlacklist:
   255→  - group: ''
   256→    kind: ResourceQuota
   257→  - group: ''
   258→    kind: LimitRange
   259→
   260→  # RBAC roles
   261→  roles:
   262→  - name: developer
   263→    description: Developers can sync apps
   264→    policies:
   265→    - p, proj:production:developer, applications, sync, production/*, allow
   266→    - p, proj:production:developer, applications, get, production/*, allow
   267→    groups:
   268→    - developers
   269→
   270→  - name: admin
   271→    description: Admins have full access
   272→    policies:
   273→    - p, proj:production:admin, applications, *, production/*, allow
   274→    groups:
   275→    - platform-team
   276→
   277→  # Sync windows
   278→  syncWindows:
   279→  - kind: allow
   280→    schedule: '0 9 * * 1-5'  # 9 AM weekdays
   281→    duration: 8h
   282→    applications:
   283→    - '*'
   284→  - kind: deny
   285→    schedule: '0 0 * * 0,6'  # Weekends
   286→    duration: 24h
   287→    applications:
   288→    - '*'
   289→
   290→  # Orphaned resources
   291→  orphanedResources:
   292→    warn: true
   293→```
   294→
   295→### ApplicationSet
   296→
   297→**Git Generator (Multi-Environment):**
   298→```yaml
   299→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   300→kind: ApplicationSet
   301→metadata:
   302→  name: myapp-environments
   303→  namespace: argocd
   304→spec:
   305→  generators:
   306→  - git:
   307→      repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   308→      revision: main
   309→      directories:
   310→      - path: k8s/overlays/*
   311→
   312→  template:
   313→    metadata:
   314→      name: 'myapp-{{path.basename}}'
   315→    spec:
   316→      project: production
   317→      source:
   318→        repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   319→        targetRevision: main
   320→        path: '{{path}}'
   321→      destination:
   322→        server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   323→        namespace: '{{path.basename}}'
   324→      syncPolicy:
   325→        automated:
   326→          prune: true
   327→          selfHeal: true
   328→```
   329→
   330→**List Generator (Multi-Cluster):**
   331→```yaml
   332→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   333→kind: ApplicationSet
   334→metadata:
   335→  name: myapp-clusters
   336→  namespace: argocd
   337→spec:
   338→  generators:
   339→  - list:
   340→      elements:
   341→      - cluster: us-east-1
   342→        url: https://cluster1.example.com
   343→        namespace: production
   344→      - cluster: us-west-2
   345→        url: https://cluster2.example.com
   346→        namespace: production
   347→      - cluster: eu-central-1
   348→        url: https://cluster3.example.com
   349→        namespace: production
   350→
   351→  template:
   352→    metadata:
   353→      name: 'myapp-{{cluster}}'
   354→    spec:
   355→      project: production
   356→      source:
   357→        repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   358→        targetRevision: main
   359→        path: k8s/overlays/production
   360→      destination:
   361→        server: '{{url}}'
   362→        namespace: '{{namespace}}'
   363→      syncPolicy:
   364→        automated:
   365→          prune: true
   366→          selfHeal: true
   367→```
   368→
   369→**Matrix Generator (Environments × Clusters):**
   370→```yaml
   371→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   372→kind: ApplicationSet
   373→metadata:
   374→  name: myapp-matrix
   375→  namespace: argocd
   376→spec:
   377→  generators:
   378→  - matrix:
   379→      generators:
   380→      - git:
   381→          repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   382→          revision: main
   383→          directories:
   384→          - path: k8s/overlays/*
   385→      - list:
   386→          elements:
   387→          - cluster: prod-us
   388→            url: https://prod-us.example.com
   389→          - cluster: prod-eu
   390→            url: https://prod-eu.example.com
   391→
   392→  template:
   393→    metadata:
   394→      name: 'myapp-{{path.basename}}-{{cluster}}'
   395→    spec:
   396→      project: production
   397→      source:
   398→        repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   399→        targetRevision: main
   400→        path: '{{path}}'
   401→      destination:
   402→        server: '{{url}}'
   403→        namespace: '{{path.basename}}'
   404→      syncPolicy:
   405→        automated:
   406→          prune: true
   407→          selfHeal: true
   408→```
   409→
   410→### Sync Strategies
   411→
   412→**Automatic Sync with Policies:**
   413→```yaml
   414→syncPolicy:
   415→  automated:
   416→    prune: true        # Delete resources not in Git
   417→    selfHeal: true     # Force sync on drift
   418→    allowEmpty: false  # Prevent deletion of all resources
   419→
   420→  syncOptions:
   421→  - CreateNamespace=true
   422→  - PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground
   423→  - PruneLast=true
   424→  - ApplyOutOfSyncOnly=true
   425→  - RespectIgnoreDifferences=true
   426→  - ServerSideApply=true
   427→
   428→  retry:
   429→    limit: 5
   430→    backoff:
   431→      duration: 5s
   432→      factor: 2
   433→      maxDuration: 3m
   434→```
   435→
   436→**Sync Hooks:**
   437→```yaml
   438→apiVersion: batch/v1
   439→kind: Job
   440→metadata:
   441→  name: database-migration
   442→  annotations:
   443→    argocd.argoproj.io/hook: PreSync
   444→    argocd.argoproj.io/hook-delete-policy: HookSucceeded
   445→    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "1"
   446→spec:
   447→  template:
   448→    spec:
   449→      containers:
   450→      - name: migration
   451→        image: myapp:latest
   452→        command: ["./migrate.sh"]
   453→      restartPolicy: Never
   454→---
   455→apiVersion: batch/v1
   456→kind: Job
   457→metadata:
   458→  name: smoke-test
   459→  annotations:
   460→    argocd.argoproj.io/hook: PostSync
   461→    argocd.argoproj.io/hook-delete-policy: BeforeHookCreation
   462→    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "5"
   463→spec:
   464→  template:
   465→    spec:
   466→      containers:
   467→      - name: test
   468→        image: curlimages/curl:latest
   469→        command: ["curl", "http://myapp/health"]
   470→      restartPolicy: Never
   471→```
   472→
   473→### SSO Configuration
   474→
   475→**Dex with GitHub:**
   476→```yaml
   477→apiVersion: v1
   478→kind: ConfigMap
   479→metadata:
   480→  name: argocd-cm
   481→  namespace: argocd
   482→data:
   483→  url: https://argocd.example.com
   484→  dex.config: |
   485→    connectors:
   486→    - type: github
   487→      id: github
   488→      name: GitHub
   489→      config:
   490→        clientID: $dex.github.clientId
   491→        clientSecret: $dex.github.clientSecret
   492→        orgs:
   493→        - name: myorg
   494→          teams:
   495→          - platform-team
   496→          - developers
   497→---
   498→apiVersion: v1
   499→kind: ConfigMap
   500→metadata:
   501→  name: argocd-rbac-cm
   502→  namespace: argocd
   503→data:
   504→  policy.default: role:readonly
   505→  policy.csv: |
   506→    # Admins have full access
   507→    g, myorg:platform-team, role:admin
   508→
   509→    # Developers can sync apps
   510→    g, myorg:developers, role:developer
   511→
   512→    # Developer role definition
   513→    p, role:developer, applications, get, */*, allow
   514→    p, role:developer, applications, sync, */*, allow
   515→    p, role:developer, repositories, get, *, allow
   516→    p, role:developer, projects, get, *, allow
   517→
   518→  scopes: '[groups, email]'
   519→```
   520→
   521→### Health Checks
   522→
   523→**Custom Health Check:**
   524→```yaml
   525→apiVersion: v1
   526→kind: ConfigMap
   527→metadata:
   528→  name: argocd-cm
   529→  namespace: argocd
   530→data:
   531→  resource.customizations.health.argoproj.io_Rollout: |
   532→    hs = {}
   533→    if obj.status ~= nil then
   534→      if obj.status.conditions ~= nil then
   535→        for i, condition in ipairs(obj.status.conditions) do
   536→          if condition.type == "Progressing" and condition.reason == "RolloutCompleted" then
   537→            hs.status = "Healthy"
   538→            hs.message = "Rollout completed"
   539→            return hs
   540→          end
   541→        end
   542→      end
   543→    end
   544→    hs.status = "Progressing"
   545→    hs.message = "Rollout in progress"
   546→    return hs
   547→```
   548→
   549→## argocd CLI Commands
   550→
   551→**Application Management:**
   552→```bash
   553→# Create application
   554→argocd app create myapp \
   555→  --repo https://github.com/myorg/myapp \
   556→  --path k8s/overlays/production \
   557→  --dest-server https://kubernetes.default.svc \
   558→  --dest-namespace production
   559→
   560→# List applications
   561→argocd app list
   562→argocd app list -o wide
   563→
   564→# Get application details
   565→argocd app get myapp
   566→argocd app get myapp --refresh
   567→
   568→# Sync application
   569→argocd app sync myapp
   570→argocd app sync myapp --prune
   571→argocd app sync myapp --dry-run
   572→argocd app sync myapp --force
   573→
   574→# Rollback
   575→argocd app rollback myapp
   576→
   577→# Delete application
   578→argocd app delete myapp
   579→argocd app delete myapp --cascade=false  # Keep resources
   580→```
   581→
   582→**Repository Management:**
   583→```bash
   584→# Add repository
   585→argocd repo add https://github.com/myorg/myapp \
   586→  --username myuser \
   587→  --password mytoken
   588→
   589→# List repositories
   590→argocd repo list
   591→
   592→# Remove repository
   593→argocd repo rm https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   594→```
   595→
   596→**Cluster Management:**
   597→```bash
   598→# Add cluster
   599→argocd cluster add my-cluster-context
   600→
   601→# List clusters
   602→argocd cluster list
   603→
   604→# Remove cluster
   605→argocd cluster rm https://cluster.example.com
   606→```
   607→
   608→**Project Management:**
   609→```bash
   610→# Create project
   611→argocd proj create production
   612→
   613→# Add repository to project
   614→argocd proj add-source production https://github.com/myorg/*
   615→
   616→# Add destination to project
   617→argocd proj add-destination production \
   618→  https://kubernetes.default.svc \
   619→  production
   620→
   621→# List projects
   622→argocd proj list
   623→
   624→# Get project details
   625→argocd proj get production
   626→```
   627→
   628→## Best Practices
   629→
   630→### 1. Use AppProjects
   631→```yaml
   632→# Separate projects by team/environment
   633→- production
   634→- staging
   635→- development
   636→```
   637→
   638→### 2. Enable Auto-Sync with Pruning
   639→```yaml
   640→syncPolicy:
   641→  automated:
   642→    prune: true
   643→    selfHeal: true
   644→```
   645→
   646→### 3. Use Sync Waves
   647→```yaml
   648→annotations:
   649→  argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "1"  # Deploy order
   650→```
   651→
   652→### 4. Implement Health Checks
   653→```yaml
   654→# Custom health checks for CRDs
   655→resource.customizations.health.<group>_<kind>
   656→```
   657→
   658→### 5. Use Sync Windows
   659→```yaml
   660→# Control deployment times
   661→syncWindows:
   662→- kind: allow
   663→  schedule: '0 9 * * 1-5'  # Business hours
   664→  duration: 8h
   665→```
   666→
   667→### 6. Enable Notifications
   668→```bash
   669→# Slack, Teams, email notifications
   670→argocd admin notifications controller
   671→```
   672→
   673→### 7. Use ApplicationSets
   674→```yaml
   675→# Manage multiple apps declaratively
   676→kind: ApplicationSet
   677→```
   678→
   679→## Anti-Patterns
   680→
   681→**1. No Resource Pruning:**
   682→```yaml
   683→# BAD: Orphaned resources
   684→automated: {}
   685→
   686→# GOOD: Enable pruning
   687→automated:
   688→  prune: true
   689→```
   690→
   691→**2. Manual Sync Only:**
   692→```yaml
   693→# BAD: Requires manual intervention
   694→syncPolicy: {}
   695→
   696→# GOOD: Automated sync
   697→syncPolicy:
   698→  automated:
   699→    prune: true
   700→    selfHeal: true
   701→```
   702→
   703→**3. Single Giant Application:**
   704→```yaml
   705→# BAD: One app for everything
   706→# GOOD: Separate apps by component/service
   707→```
   708→
   709→**4. No RBAC:**
   710→```yaml
   711→# GOOD: Always implement project-level RBAC
   712→roles:
   713→- name: developer
   714→  policies:
   715→  - p, proj:prod:dev, applications, sync, prod/*, allow
   716→```
   717→
   718→## Approach
   719→
   720→When implementing ArgoCD:
   721→
   722→1. **Start Simple**: Deploy one application first
   723→2. **GitOps Everything**: All config in Git
   724→3. **Automate**: Enable auto-sync and self-heal
   725→4. **Organize**: Use AppProjects for isolation
   726→5. **RBAC**: Implement least-privilege access
   727→6. **Monitor**: Set up notifications and alerts
   728→7. **Scale**: Use ApplicationSets for multi-cluster/multi-env
   729→8. **Security**: Enable SSO and audit logging
   730→
   731→Always design GitOps workflows that are declarative, auditable, and automated following cloud-native principles.
   732→

   734→
   735→- ArgoCD Documentation: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/
   736→- GitOps Principles: https://opengitops.dev/
   737→- ApplicationSet: https://argocd-applicationset.readthedocs.io/
   738→- ArgoCD Notifications: https://argocd-notifications.readthedocs.io/

1→apiVersion: apps/v1
     2→kind: StatefulSet
     3→metadata:

     5→  labels:
     6→    app: postgres
     7→    app.kubernetes.io/name: postgres
     8→    app.kubernetes.io/component: database
     9→    app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
    10→spec:
    11→  serviceName: postgres
    12→  replicas: 1
    13→  selector:
    14→    matchLabels:
    15→      app: postgres
    16→      app.kubernetes.io/name: postgres
    17→      app.kubernetes.io/component: database
    18→      app.kubernetes.io/part-of: micro-commerce
    19→  template:
    20→    metadata:
    21→      labels:
    22→        app: postgres
    23→        app.kubernetes.io/name: postgres
    24→        app.kubernetes.io/component: database

    26→    spec:
    27→      serviceAccountName: postgres

    29→      - name: postgres
    30→        securityContext:
    31→          runAsNonRoot: false

    33→          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
    34→          capabilities:
    35→            drop:

    37→        image: postgres:17

    41→        env:
    42→        - name: POSTGRES_USER
    43→          valueFrom:

    46→              key: username
    47→        - name: POSTGRES_PASSWORD
    48→          valueFrom:
    49→            secretKeyRef:
    50→              name: postgres-credentials
    51→              key: password
    52→        - name: POSTGRES_DB
    53→          value: appdb

    56→        volumeMounts:

    59→        readinessProbe:

    62→          initialDelaySeconds: 5

    67→          initialDelaySeconds: 15

    69→        resources:
    70→          requests:

1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
     5→PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
     6→CLUSTER_NAME="micro-commerce"
     7→NAMESPACE="micro-commerce"
     8→SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION="v0.27.3"
     9→ARGOCD_VERSION="v3.3.2"
    10→
    11→# Color output
    12→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    13→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    14→RED='\033[0;31m'
    15→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    16→
    17→info() { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    18→warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    19→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*"; }
    20→
    21→# --- Pre-flight: Check required CLI tools ---
    22→check_prerequisites() {
    23→  local missing=false
    24→  for cmd in kind kubectl kubeseal docker; do
    25→    if ! command -v "$cmd" &>/dev/null; then
    26→      error "Required tool '$cmd' not found. Please install it before running this script."
    27→      missing=true
    28→    fi
    29→  done
    30→  if [ "$missing" = true ]; then
    31→    error "Install missing tools:"
    32→    error "  kind:      https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/quick-start/#installation"
    33→    error "  kubectl:   https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/tools/"
    34→    error "  kubeseal:  https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets#kubeseal"
    35→    error "  docker:    https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/"
    36→    exit 1
    37→  fi
    38→  info "All required tools found."
    39→}
    40→
    41→check_prerequisites
    42→
    43→# --- Trap handler: Print cleanup guidance on failure ---
    44→cleanup_on_error() {
    45→  echo ""
    46→  error "Bootstrap failed! To clean up:"
    47→  error "  kind delete cluster --name ${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    48→  error "  kubectl config unset contexts.kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    49→  echo ""
    50→  error "To retry, run this script again."
    51→}
    52→
    53→trap cleanup_on_error ERR
    54→
    55→# --- Step 1: Create kind cluster ---
    56→if kind get clusters 2>/dev/null | grep -q "^${CLUSTER_NAME}$"; then
    57→  info "Cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}' already exists, skipping creation."
    58→else
    59→  info "Creating kind cluster '${CLUSTER_NAME}'..."
    60→  kind create cluster --name "$CLUSTER_NAME" --config "$SCRIPT_DIR/kind-config.yaml"
    61→fi
    62→
    63→# --- Step 2: Verify kubectl context targets the kind cluster ---
    64→CURRENT_CONTEXT=$(kubectl config current-context 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    65→EXPECTED_CONTEXT="kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    66→if [ "$CURRENT_CONTEXT" != "$EXPECTED_CONTEXT" ]; then
    67→  error "kubectl context is '${CURRENT_CONTEXT}' but expected '${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}'"
    68→  error "This script only runs against the local kind cluster."
    69→  error "Switch context: kubectl config use-context ${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}"
    70→  exit 1
    71→fi
    72→info "kubectl context verified: ${EXPECTED_CONTEXT}"
    73→kubectl config use-context "kind-${CLUSTER_NAME}"
    74→
    75→# --- Step 3: Install SealedSecrets controller ---
    76→info "Installing SealedSecrets controller ${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}..."
    77→kubectl apply -f "https://github.com/bitnami-labs/sealed-secrets/releases/download/${SEALED_SECRETS_VERSION}/controller.yaml"
    78→
    79→# --- Step 4: Wait for SealedSecrets controller ---
    80→info "Waiting for SealedSecrets controller to be ready..."
    81→kubectl rollout status deployment/sealed-secrets-controller -n kube-system --timeout=120s
    82→
    83→# --- Step 5: Create namespace ---
    84→info "Creating namespace '${NAMESPACE}'..."
    85→kubectl create namespace "$NAMESPACE" --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    86→
    87→# --- Step 6: Seal dev secrets and apply directly ---
    88→info "Sealing dev secrets..."
    89→
    90→seal_secret() {
    91→  local name="$1"
    92→  local output="$2"
    93→  shift 2
    94→  # Remaining args are --from-literal pairs
    95→
    96→  kubectl create secret generic "$name" \
    97→    --namespace "$NAMESPACE" \
    98→    "$@" \
    99→    --dry-run=client -o yaml | \
   100→  kubeseal \
   101→    --controller-name=sealed-secrets-controller \
   102→    --controller-namespace=kube-system \

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Create AppProject and add sync waves, retry, ignoreDifferences to all ArgoCD apps</name>
  <files>
    infra/k8s/argocd/project.yaml,
    infra/k8s/argocd/root-app.yaml,

  </files>
  <action>
1. Create `infra/k8s/argocd/project.yaml` — a dedicated ArgoCD AppProject:
   ```yaml
   apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   kind: AppProject
   metadata:
     name: micro-commerce
     namespace: argocd
   spec:
     description: MicroCommerce application project
     sourceRepos:
     - https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
     destinations:
     - namespace: micro-commerce
       server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
     - namespace: argocd
       server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
     clusterResourceWhitelist:
     - group: ''
       kind: Namespace
   ```
   The destinations allow micro-commerce namespace (workloads) and argocd namespace (for the root app's child Application resources). clusterResourceWhitelist allows Namespace only (needed by CreateNamespace=true sync option).

2. Update `infra/k8s/argocd/root-app.yaml`:
   - Change `project: default` to `project: micro-commerce`
   - Add retry strategy matching child apps:
     ```yaml
     retry:
       limit: 5
       backoff:
         duration: 5s
         factor: 2
         maxDuration: 3m
     ```
   - Add sync-wave annotation `"0"` (deploys first to create child apps)

3. Add sync-wave annotations to ALL 8 child Application manifests in `infra/k8s/argocd/apps/`. Use this ordering:
   - Wave 1: postgres (database must be first)
   - Wave 2: rabbitmq, keycloak (depend on nothing but should wait for DB)
   - Wave 3: apiservice (depends on postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak)
   - Wave 4: gateway (depends on apiservice)
   - Wave 5: web (depends on gateway, keycloak)
   - Wave 6: otel-collector, aspire-dashboard (observability, last)

   Add to each Application's `metadata.annotations`:
   ```yaml
   annotations:
     argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "N"
   ```

4. Change `project: default` to `project: micro-commerce` in ALL 8 child Application manifests.

5. For `postgres.yaml` only, add `ignoreDifferences` and `RespectIgnoreDifferences`:
   ```yaml
   spec:
     ignoreDifferences:
     - group: apps
       kind: StatefulSet
       jsonPointers:
       - /spec/volumeClaimTemplates/0/apiVersion
       - /spec/volumeClaimTemplates/0/kind
     syncPolicy:
       syncOptions:
       - CreateNamespace=true
       - RespectIgnoreDifferences=true
   ```
   This prevents ArgoCD from reporting OutOfSync due to Kubernetes mutating volumeClaimTemplates metadata fields.

6. Also add `RespectIgnoreDifferences` and similar `ignoreDifferences` to `rabbitmq.yaml` since it is also a StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates (added in Phase 34).
  </action>

  <name>Task 1: Create placeholder sealed-secret.yaml files and add to Kustomize resources</name>
  <files>
    infra/k8s/base/postgres/sealed-secret.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/web/sealed-secret.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/postgres/kustomization.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/rabbitmq/kustomization.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/keycloak/kustomization.yaml,
    infra/k8s/base/web/kustomization.yaml
  </files>
  <action>
1. Create placeholder SealedSecret YAML files that will be overwritten by bootstrap.sh at cluster setup time. These placeholders make the repo self-contained and let ArgoCD see the SealedSecret resources declaratively.

   For each service, create `infra/k8s/base/{service}/sealed-secret.yaml` with a placeholder SealedSecret. Use this template pattern:

   **postgres/sealed-secret.yaml:**
   ```yaml
   # This file is overwritten by bootstrap.sh with cluster-specific sealed values.
   # The placeholder structure lets ArgoCD track the resource declaratively.
   apiVersion: bitnami.com/v1alpha1
   kind: SealedSecret
   metadata:
     name: postgres-credentials
     annotations:
       sealedsecrets.bitnami.com/managed: "true"
   spec:
     encryptedData:
       username: AgAAAA==
       password: AgAAAA==
     template:
       metadata:
         name: postgres-credentials
   ```

   **rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml:**
   Same structure, name: `rabbitmq-credentials`, keys: `username`, `password`

   **keycloak/sealed-secret.yaml:**
   Same structure, name: `keycloak-credentials`, keys: `admin-username`, `admin-password`

   **web/sealed-secret.yaml:**
   Same structure, name: `web-secrets`, keys: `auth-secret`, `keycloak-client-secret`

   IMPORTANT: The `AgAAAA==` is a dummy encrypted value. The actual encrypted values are generated per-cluster by `kubeseal` in bootstrap.sh. These placeholders exist solely so the file is in Git and kustomize can reference it.

2. Add `sealed-secret.yaml` to the `resources` list in each base kustomization.yaml:

   For `infra/k8s/base/postgres/kustomization.yaml`, add `- sealed-secret.yaml` to resources (after serviceaccount.yaml).
   Same for rabbitmq, keycloak, and web base kustomization.yaml files.
  </action>

  </verify>

156→### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
   157→**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
   158→**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
   159→**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
   160→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   161→  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
   162→  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
   163→  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
   164→  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
   165→  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
   166→  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces

# Roadmap: MicroCommerce

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 MVP** — Phases 1-10 (shipped 2026-02-13) — [archive](milestones/v1.0-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v1.1 User Features** — Phases 11-14.3 (shipped 2026-02-14) — [archive](milestones/v1.1-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v2.0 DDD Foundation** — Phases 15-22 (shipped 2026-02-25) — [archive](milestones/v2.0-ROADMAP.md)
- ✅ **v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps** — Phases 23-29 (shipped 2026-03-03) — [archive](milestones/v3.0-ROADMAP.md)
- 🚧 **v3.1 K8s & GitOps Hardening** — Phases 30-35 (in progress)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 MVP (Phases 1-10) — SHIPPED 2026-02-13</summary>

- [x] Phase 1: Foundation & Project Structure (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-01-30
- [x] Phase 2: Catalog Domain & Admin CRUD (7/7 plans) — completed 2026-01-30
- [x] Phase 3: Catalog Storefront & Seed Data (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-02-07
- [x] Phase 4: Inventory Domain (5/5 plans) — completed 2026-02-08
- [x] Phase 5: Event Bus Infrastructure (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-09
- [x] Phase 6: Cart Domain (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-09
- [x] Phase 7: Ordering Domain & Checkout (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-10
- [x] Phase 8: Order History & Management (5/5 plans) — completed 2026-02-12
- [x] Phase 9: API Gateway (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-12
- [x] Phase 10: Testing & Polish (6/6 plans) — completed 2026-02-13

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.1 User Features (Phases 11-14.3) — SHIPPED 2026-02-14</summary>

- [x] Phase 11: User Profiles & Authentication Flow (5/5 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 12: Product Reviews & Ratings (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 13: Wishlists & Saved Items (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-13
- [x] Phase 14: Integration & Polish (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.1: Check DDD Approach Correctness (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.2: ValueObject Record Struct Migration (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 14.3: DDD Audit Issue Fixes (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-14

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v2.0 DDD Foundation (Phases 15-22) — SHIPPED 2026-02-25</summary>

- [x] Phase 15: Foundation - Entity Base & Audit Infrastructure (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-14
- [x] Phase 16: Conventions - DRY Configuration (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24
- [x] Phase 16.1: Adopt Vogen for Value Object (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24
- [x] Phase 17: Result Pattern - Explicit Error Handling (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24
- [x] Phase 18: Enumeration - Enums with Behavior (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24
- [x] Phase 19: Specification Pattern - Complex Query Logic (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-24
- [x] Phase 20: Integration Testing Infrastructure (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-02-25
- [x] Phase 21: Adoption - Full Building Block Integration (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-25
- [x] Phase 22: Wire Interceptors to DbContexts (1/1 plan) — completed 2026-02-25

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (Phases 23-29) — SHIPPED 2026-03-03</summary>

- [x] Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-25
- [x] Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-02-26
- [x] Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-02-26
- [x] Phase 25.1: Update UI with shadcn (12/12 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure (1/1 plan) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 28: Observability (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 29: K8s Client-Side API & Bootstrap Polish (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03

</details>

### v3.1 K8s & GitOps Hardening

**Milestone Goal:** Fix critical security, reliability, and CI/CD issues identified in the v3.0 implementation audit.

- [x] **Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes** - Restore broken workflows with correct SDK, paths, and test gates (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening** - Secure and optimize CI with permissions, caching, and path filters (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene** - Clean up K8s manifests with standard labels, image policies, and overlay structure (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening** - Lock down workloads with security contexts, service accounts, and sealed secrets (completed 2026-03-08)
- [x] **Phase 34: Reliability Improvements** - Harden runtime with persistent messaging, startup probes, and bootstrap safety (completed 2026-03-08)
- [ ] **Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices** - Enforce deployment ordering, seal secrets in Git, and scope ArgoCD access

## Phase Details

### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
**Goal**: CI workflows pass on push to master — tests run, images build, releases work
**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase — CI is broken, fix it first)
**Requirements**: CICD-01, CICD-02, CICD-03, CICD-07, CICD-08
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. `dotnet-test.yml` workflow passes with .NET 10 SDK and current project paths
  2. `release.yml` builds and publishes images using correct project references
  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing
  4. Dockerfile uses ARG for build-time placeholder secrets (no ENV leaking to runtime)
  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 30-01-PLAN.md — Fix all CI/CD workflows (SDK, paths, test gate, Aspire, Dockerfile secrets)

### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
**Depends on**: Phase 30
**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows

### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads

### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay

### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
**Plans:** 3/3 plans complete
Plans:
- [ ] 34-01-PLAN.md — Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler)
- [ ] 34-02-PLAN.md — Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, and startup probes
- [ ] 34-03-PLAN.md — MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts

### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces
**Plans:** 2 plans
Plans:
- [ ] 35-01-PLAN.md — AppProject, sync waves, root app retry, and PostgreSQL ignoreDifferences
- [ ] 35-02-PLAN.md — Sealed secrets in Git as Kustomize resources and consistent overlay paths

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|-------|--------|-----------|
| 1-10 | v1.0 | 49/49 | Complete | 2026-02-13 |
| 11-14.3 | v1.1 | 23/23 | Complete | 2026-02-14 |
| 15-22 | v2.0 | 9/9 | Complete | 2026-02-25 |
| 23-29 | v3.0 | 29/29 | Complete | 2026-03-03 |
| 30. CI/CD Pipeline Fixes | 1/1 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 31. CI/CD Hardening | 1/1 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 32. Kustomize Hygiene | 2/2 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 33. K8s Security Hardening | 2/2 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 34. Reliability Improvements | 3/3 | Complete    | 2026-03-08 | - |
| 35. ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices | v3.1 | 0/2 | Not started | - |

---
*Roadmap created: 2026-01-29*
*v1.0 shipped: 2026-02-13*
*v1.1 shipped: 2026-02-14*
*v2.0 shipped: 2026-02-25*
*v3.0 shipped: 2026-03-03*

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 35-argocd-gitops-best-practices
**Plans:** 2 plan(s) in 1 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 35-01, 35-02 | yes, yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files | Requirements |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|--------------|
| 35-01 | AppProject, sync waves, root app retry, PostgreSQL ignoreDifferences | 2 | 11 (ArgoCD apps + project + bootstrap.sh) | ARGO-01, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, SEC-05 |
| 35-02 | Sealed secrets in Git + consistent overlay paths for all services | 2 | 12 (overlays + base kustomizations + sealed-secret YAMLs) | ARGO-02, ARGO-05 |

### Requirement Coverage

| Requirement | Plan |
|-------------|------|
| ARGO-01 (Sync waves) | 35-01 |
| ARGO-02 (Sealed secrets in Git) | 35-02 |
| ARGO-03 (Root app retry) | 35-01 |
| ARGO-04 (PostgreSQL ignoreDifferences) | 35-01 |
| ARGO-05 (Consistent overlay paths) | 35-02 |
| SEC-05 (Dedicated AppProject) | 35-01 |

### Key Design Decisions

- **Sync wave ordering:** postgres(1) -> rabbitmq/keycloak(2) -> apiservice(3) -> gateway(4) -> web(5) -> otel/aspire(6)
- **Sealed secret placeholders:** Committed with dummy `AgAAAA==` encrypted data; bootstrap.sh overwrites with real cluster-sealed values
- **AppProject scope:** Restricts to single sourceRepo (`baotoq/micro-commerce.git`) and two namespaces (`micro-commerce` for workloads, `argocd` for child Application CRDs)
- **Both plans are Wave 1 (parallel):** Plan 01 touches ArgoCD app metadata/spec fields; Plan 02 touches overlay structure and source.path. Note in Plan 02 that both plans modify the same ArgoCD app files -- if running sequentially, Plan 02 reads files as modified by Plan 01

### Files Created

- `/Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce/.planning/phases/35-argocd-gitops-best-practices/35-01-PLAN.md`
- `/Users/baotoq/Work/micro-commerce/.planning/phases/35-argocd-gitops-best-practices/35-02-PLAN.md`

### Next Steps

`/clear` first - fresh context window

   128→  <action>
   129→1. Create `infra/k8s/argocd/project.yaml` — a dedicated ArgoCD AppProject:
   130→   ```yaml
   131→   apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   132→   kind: AppProject
   133→   metadata:
   134→     name: micro-commerce
   135→     namespace: argocd
   136→   spec:
   137→     description: MicroCommerce application project
   138→     sourceRepos:
   139→     - https://github.com/baotoq/micro-commerce.git
   140→     destinations:
   141→     - namespace: micro-commerce
   142→       server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   143→     - namespace: argocd
   144→       server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   145→     clusterResourceWhitelist:
   146→     - group: ''
   147→       kind: Namespace
   148→   ```
   149→   The destinations allow micro-commerce namespace (workloads) and argocd namespace (for the root app's child Application resources). clusterResourceWhitelist allows Namespace only (needed by CreateNamespace=true sync option).
   150→
   151→2. Update `infra/k8s/argocd/root-app.yaml`:
   152→   - Change `project: default` to `project: micro-commerce`
   153→   - Add retry strategy matching child apps:
   154→     ```yaml
   155→     retry:
   156→       limit: 5
   157→       backoff:
   158→         duration: 5s
   159→         factor: 2
   160→         maxDuration: 3m
   161→     ```
   162→   - Add sync-wave annotation `"0"` (deploys first to create child apps)
   163→
   164→3. Add sync-wave annotations to ALL 8 child Application manifests in `infra/k8s/argocd/apps/`. Use this ordering:
   165→   - Wave 1: postgres (database must be first)
   166→   - Wave 2: rabbitmq, keycloak (depend on nothing but should wait for DB)
   167→   - Wave 3: apiservice (depends on postgres, rabbitmq, keycloak)
   168→   - Wave 4: gateway (depends on apiservice)
   169→   - Wave 5: web (depends on gateway, keycloak)
   170→   - Wave 6: otel-collector, aspire-dashboard (observability, last)
   171→
   172→   Add to each Application's `metadata.annotations`:
   173→   ```yaml
   174→   annotations:
   175→     argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "N"
   176→   ```
   177→
   178→4. Change `project: default` to `project: micro-commerce` in ALL 8 child Application manifests.
   179→
   180→5. For `postgres.yaml` only, add `ignoreDifferences` and `RespectIgnoreDifferences`:
   181→   ```yaml
   182→   spec:
   183→     ignoreDifferences:
   184→     - group: apps
   185→       kind: StatefulSet
   186→       jsonPointers:
   187→       - /spec/volumeClaimTemplates/0/apiVersion
   188→       - /spec/volumeClaimTemplates/0/kind
   189→     syncPolicy:
   190→       syncOptions:
   191→       - CreateNamespace=true
   192→       - RespectIgnoreDifferences=true
   193→   ```
   194→   This prevents ArgoCD from reporting OutOfSync due to Kubernetes mutating volumeClaimTemplates metadata fields.
   195→
   196→6. Also add `RespectIgnoreDifferences` and similar `ignoreDifferences` to `rabbitmq.yaml` since it is also a StatefulSet with volumeClaimTemplates (added in Phase 34).
   197→  </action>

   127→1. Create placeholder SealedSecret YAML files that will be overwritten by bootstrap.sh at cluster setup time. These placeholders make the repo self-contained and let ArgoCD see the SealedSecret resources declaratively.
   128→
   129→   For each service, create `infra/k8s/base/{service}/sealed-secret.yaml` with a placeholder SealedSecret. Use this template pattern:
   130→
   131→   **postgres/sealed-secret.yaml:**
   132→   ```yaml
   133→   # This file is overwritten by bootstrap.sh with cluster-specific sealed values.
   134→   # The placeholder structure lets ArgoCD track the resource declaratively.
   135→   apiVersion: bitnami.com/v1alpha1
   136→   kind: SealedSecret
   137→   metadata:
   138→     name: postgres-credentials
   139→     annotations:
   140→       sealedsecrets.bitnami.com/managed: "true"
   141→   spec:
   142→     encryptedData:
   143→       username: AgAAAA==
   144→       password: AgAAAA==
   145→     template:
   146→       metadata:
   147→         name: postgres-credentials
   148→   ```
   149→
   150→   **rabbitmq/sealed-secret.yaml:**

    72→
    73→**Milestone Goal:** Fix critical security, reliability, and CI/CD issues identified in the v3.0 implementation audit.

    78→- [x] **Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening** - Lock down workloads with security contexts, service accounts, and sealed secrets (completed 2026-03-08)
    79→- [x] **Phase 34: Reliability Improvements** - Harden runtime with persistent messaging, startup probes, and bootstrap safety (completed 2026-03-08)
    80→- [ ] **Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices** - Enforce deployment ordering, seal secrets in Git, and scope ArgoCD access
    81→
    82→## Phase Details
    83→
    84→### Phase 30: CI/CD Pipeline Fixes
    85→**Goal**: CI workflows pass on push to master — tests run, images build, releases work
    86→**Depends on**: Nothing (first phase — CI is broken, fix it first)
    87→**Requirements**: CICD-01, CICD-02, CICD-03, CICD-07, CICD-08
    88→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    89→  1. `dotnet-test.yml` workflow passes with .NET 10 SDK and current project paths
    90→  2. `release.yml` builds and publishes images using correct project references
    91→  3. Tests run as a required gate before release publishing
    92→  4. Dockerfile uses ARG for build-time placeholder secrets (no ENV leaking to runtime)
    93→  5. Aspire workload version is pinned (not floating latest)
    94→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
    95→Plans:
    96→- [ ] 30-01-PLAN.md — Fix all CI/CD workflows (SDK, paths, test gate, Aspire, Dockerfile secrets)
    97→
    98→### Phase 31: CI/CD Hardening
    99→**Goal**: CI pipelines follow security and performance best practices
   100→**Depends on**: Phase 30
   101→**Requirements**: CICD-04, CICD-05, CICD-06
   102→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   103→  1. All workflow files declare explicit least-privilege `permissions:` blocks
   104→  2. NuGet packages are cached across workflow runs (faster builds)
   105→  3. Container image builds skip when only non-source files change (docs, manifests)
   106→**Plans:** 1/1 plans complete
   107→Plans:
   108→- [ ] 31-01-PLAN.md — Add permissions, NuGet caching, and path filters to all workflows
   109→
   110→### Phase 32: Kustomize Hygiene
   111→**Goal**: K8s manifests follow Kustomize conventions and Kubernetes labeling standards
   112→**Depends on**: Nothing (independent of CI/CD fixes)
   113→**Requirements**: KUST-01, KUST-02, KUST-03, KUST-04
   114→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   115→  1. No hardcoded `namespace:` fields in base manifests — Kustomize transformer sets namespace
   116→  2. otel-collector and aspire-dashboard appear in dev overlay (not base)
   117→  3. All application deployments declare explicit `imagePullPolicy`
   118→  4. All workloads carry standard `app.kubernetes.io/*` labels (name, component, part-of)
   119→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   120→Plans:
   121→- [ ] 32-01-PLAN.md — Remove hardcoded namespaces and move dev-only resources to overlay
   122→- [ ] 32-02-PLAN.md — Add standard Kubernetes labels and imagePullPolicy to all workloads
   123→
   124→### Phase 33: K8s Security Hardening
   125→**Goal**: All workloads run with least-privilege security posture
   126→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (clean manifests before adding security)
   127→**Requirements**: SEC-01, SEC-02, SEC-03, SEC-04
   128→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   129→  1. Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) are SealedSecrets, not plain Secrets
   130→  2. All 8 workloads have securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities
   131→  3. Keycloak base manifest runs `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
   132→  4. Each workload has its own ServiceAccount with automountServiceAccountToken: false
   133→**Plans:** 2/2 plans complete
   134→Plans:
   135→- [ ] 33-01-PLAN.md — Security contexts and dedicated ServiceAccounts for all 8 workloads
   136→- [ ] 33-02-PLAN.md — Web SealedSecrets and Keycloak production mode with dev overlay
   137→
   138→### Phase 34: Reliability Improvements
   139→**Goal**: Services survive restarts, start reliably, and bootstrap is safe to run
   140→**Depends on**: Phase 32 (manifests cleaned up before adding probes/volumes)
   141→**Requirements**: REL-01, REL-02, REL-03, REL-04, REL-05, REL-06, REL-07
   142→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   143→  1. Client-side API calls in K8s reach the gateway via browser-reachable URL (not cluster-internal)
   144→  2. RabbitMQ runs as StatefulSet with PersistentVolumeClaim — messages survive pod restarts
   145→  3. Gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, and aspire-dashboard have startup probes
   146→  4. Bootstrap script exits early with clear error if kind/kubectl/kubeseal CLI tools are missing
   147→  5. Bootstrap script has trap handler that prints cleanup guidance on failure
   148→  6. Bootstrap script refuses to run if kubectl context is not the expected kind cluster
   149→  7. MassTransit outbox is registered on all DbContexts that publish domain events
   150→**Plans:** 3/3 plans complete
   151→Plans:
   152→- [ ] 34-01-PLAN.md — Bootstrap script safety (pre-flight checks, context guard, trap handler)
   153→- [ ] 34-02-PLAN.md — Client-side API URL fix, RabbitMQ StatefulSet, and startup probes
   154→- [ ] 34-03-PLAN.md — MassTransit outbox registration for all domain-event DbContexts
   155→
   156→### Phase 35: ArgoCD GitOps Best Practices
   157→**Goal**: ArgoCD deployments are ordered, scoped, and fully declarative (sealed secrets in Git)
   158→**Depends on**: Phase 33 (sealed secrets must exist before committing to Git)
   159→**Requirements**: ARGO-01, ARGO-02, ARGO-03, ARGO-04, ARGO-05, SEC-05
   160→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   161→  1. Sync wave annotations enforce infrastructure-before-apps deployment ordering
   162→  2. All sealed secret YAML files are committed to Git and listed as Kustomize resources
   163→  3. Root app retry strategy matches child app configuration (consistent retry behavior)
   164→  4. PostgreSQL StatefulSet diffs are ignored by ArgoCD (RespectIgnoreDifferences enabled)
   165→  5. All applications use consistent overlay paths (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
   166→  6. Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject restricts sourceRepos and destination namespaces
   167→**Plans:** 2 plans
   168→Plans:
   169→- [ ] 35-01-PLAN.md — AppProject, sync waves, root app retry, and PostgreSQL ignoreDifferences
   170→- [ ] 35-02-PLAN.md — Sealed secrets in Git as Kustomize resources and consistent overlay paths

1→# Requirements: MicroCommerce
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-08
     4→**Core Value:** A user can complete a purchase end-to-end — deployed to Kubernetes via GitOps
     5→
     6→## v3.1 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for K8s & GitOps Hardening. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### CI/CD
    11→
    12→- [x] **CICD-01**: .NET SDK version updated to 10.0.x in dotnet-test.yml and release.yml
    13→- [x] **CICD-02**: Stale project paths in release.yml updated to current structure (MicroCommerce.ApiService, MicroCommerce.Gateway)
    14→- [x] **CICD-03**: Aspire workload install pinned to specific version
    15→- [x] **CICD-04**: Explicit least-privilege `permissions:` block on all workflows
    16→- [x] **CICD-05**: NuGet package caching added to test and image build workflows
    17→- [x] **CICD-06**: Path filtering on container-images.yml to skip non-source changes
    18→- [x] **CICD-07**: Tests re-enabled as gate in release.yml
    19→- [x] **CICD-08**: Dockerfile uses ARG instead of ENV for build-time placeholder secrets
    20→
    21→### Security
    22→
    23→- [x] **SEC-01**: Web frontend secrets (AUTH_SECRET, KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET) moved to SealedSecret
    24→- [x] **SEC-02**: securityContext added to all 8 workloads (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, drop ALL capabilities)
    25→- [x] **SEC-03**: Keycloak base manifest uses `start` (production mode); dev overlay patches to `start-dev`
    26→- [x] **SEC-04**: Dedicated ServiceAccounts per workload with automountServiceAccountToken: false
    27→- [ ] **SEC-05**: Dedicated ArgoCD AppProject with restricted sourceRepos and destinations
    28→
    29→### ArgoCD
    30→
    31→- [ ] **ARGO-01**: Sync wave annotations on all Application resources enforcing dependency ordering
    32→- [ ] **ARGO-02**: Sealed secret YAML files committed to Git and added as Kustomize resources
    33→- [ ] **ARGO-03**: Root app retry strategy matching child app configuration
    34→- [ ] **ARGO-04**: ignoreDifferences configured for PostgreSQL StatefulSet with RespectIgnoreDifferences
    35→- [ ] **ARGO-05**: Consistent overlay paths for all applications (infrastructure services moved to overlays)
    36→
    37→### Reliability
    38→
    39→- [x] **REL-01**: Client-side API URL resolution returns browser-reachable URL in K8s
    40→- [x] **REL-02**: RabbitMQ converted to StatefulSet with persistent volume
    41→- [x] **REL-03**: Startup probes added to gateway, web, rabbitmq, otel-collector, aspire-dashboard
    42→- [x] **REL-04**: Bootstrap script checks for required CLI tools before execution
    43→- [x] **REL-05**: Bootstrap script adds trap handler for failure cleanup guidance
    44→- [x] **REL-06**: Bootstrap script context guard prevents applying to wrong cluster
    45→- [x] **REL-07**: MassTransit outbox extended to cover all DbContexts that publish domain events
    46→
    47→### Kustomize Hygiene
    48→
    49→- [x] **KUST-01**: Hardcoded namespaces removed from base manifests (Kustomize transformer handles it)
    50→- [x] **KUST-02**: otel-collector and aspire-dashboard added to dev overlay
    51→- [x] **KUST-03**: Explicit imagePullPolicy on all application deployments
    52→- [x] **KUST-04**: Standard Kubernetes labels (app.kubernetes.io/*) on all workloads
    53→
    54→## Future Requirements
    55→
    56→None deferred — all audit findings scoped to v3.1.
    57→
    58→## Out of Scope
    59→
    60→| Feature | Reason |
    61→|---------|--------|
    62→| Network policies | Low priority for local dev kind cluster |
    63→| PodDisruptionBudgets | Single-replica dev; add when scaling |
    64→| Pod anti-affinity/topology spread | Irrelevant for single-node kind cluster |
    65→| ResourceQuota/LimitRange | Resource limits already on all containers |
    66→| Prometheus scrape annotations | OTEL Collector handles observability |
    67→| Next.js frontend OTEL instrumentation | Separate concern, not v3.0 hardening |
    68→| Random generated credentials in bootstrap | Dev-only kind cluster; document the pattern |
    69→| Pin image digests (node, postgres, etc.) | Dependabot handles version updates |

1→---
     2→name: argocd-expert
     3→version: 1.0.0
     4→description: Expert-level ArgoCD GitOps deployment, application management, sync strategies, and production operations
     5→category: devops
     6→author: PCL Team
     7→license: Apache-2.0
     8→tags:
     9→  - argocd
    10→  - gitops
    11→  - kubernetes
    12→  - continuous-deployment
    13→  - declarative
    14→  - automation
    15→allowed-tools:
    16→  - Read
    17→  - Write
    18→  - Edit
    19→  - Bash(argocd:*, kubectl:*)
    20→  - Glob
    21→  - Grep
    22→requirements:
    23→  argocd: ">=2.9"
    24→  kubernetes: ">=1.28"
    25→---
    26→
    27→# ArgoCD Expert
    28→
    29→You are an expert in ArgoCD with deep knowledge of GitOps workflows, application deployment, sync strategies, RBAC, and production operations. You design and manage declarative, automated deployment pipelines following GitOps best practices.
    30→
    31→## Core Expertise
    32→
    33→### ArgoCD Architecture
    34→
    35→**Components:**
    36→```
    37→ArgoCD:
    38→├── API Server (UI/CLI/API)
    39→├── Repository Server (Git interaction)
    40→├── Application Controller (K8s reconciliation)
    41→├── Redis (caching)
    42→├── Dex (SSO/RBAC)
    43→└── ApplicationSet Controller (multi-cluster)
    44→```
    45→
    46→### Installation
    47→
    48→**Install ArgoCD:**
    49→```bash
    50→# Create namespace
    51→kubectl create namespace argocd
    52→
    53→# Install ArgoCD
    54→kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/install.yaml
    55→
    56→# Install with HA
    57→kubectl apply -n argocd -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/argoproj/argo-cd/stable/manifests/ha/install.yaml
    58→
    59→# Get admin password
    60→kubectl -n argocd get secret argocd-initial-admin-secret -o jsonpath="{.data.password}" | base64 -d
    61→
    62→# Port forward to access UI
    63→kubectl port-forward svc/argocd-server -n argocd 8080:443
    64→
    65→# Login via CLI
    66→argocd login localhost:8080 --username admin --password <password>
    67→
    68→# Change admin password
    69→argocd account update-password
    70→```
    71→
    72→**Production Installation with Custom Values:**
    73→```yaml
    74→# argocd-values.yaml
    75→apiVersion: v1
    76→kind: ConfigMap
    77→metadata:
    78→  name: argocd-cm
    79→  namespace: argocd
    80→data:
    81→  # Repository credentials
    82→  repositories: |
    83→    - url: https://github.com/myorg/myrepo
    84→      passwordSecret:
    85→        name: github-secret
    86→        key: password
    87→      usernameSecret:
    88→        name: github-secret
    89→        key: username
    90→
    91→  # Resource customizations
    92→  resource.customizations: |
    93→    networking.k8s.io/Ingress:
    94→      health.lua: |
    95→        hs = {}
    96→        hs.status = "Healthy"
    97→        return hs
    98→
    99→  # Timeout settings
   100→  timeout.reconciliation: 180s
   101→
   102→  # Diff customizations
   103→  resource.compareoptions: |
   104→    ignoreAggregatedRoles: true
   105→
   106→  # UI customization
   107→  ui.cssurl: "https://cdn.example.com/custom.css"
   108→```
   109→
   110→### Application CRD
   111→
   112→**Basic Application:**
   113→```yaml
   114→apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
   115→kind: Application
   116→metadata:
   117→  name: myapp
   118→  namespace: argocd
   119→  finalizers:
   120→  - resources-finalizer.argocd.argoproj.io
   121→spec:
   122→  project: production
   123→
   124→  source:
   125→    repoURL: https://github.com/myorg/myapp
   126→    targetRevision: main
   127→    path: k8s/overlays/production
   128→
   129→  destination:
   130→    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
   131→    namespace: production
   132→
   133→  syncPolicy:
